content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

In one operation, you may choose a prefix of the array of length kkk (1â‰¤kâ‰¤N1 â‰¤ k â‰¤ N1â‰¤kâ‰¤N) and an integer Xâ‰Ą0X â‰Ą 0Xâ‰Ą0, and apply the bitwiseXORbitwise XORbitwiseXOR operation to all elements in that prefix:


[Ai=AiâŠ•X for all 1â‰¤iâ‰¤k][ A_i = A_i \oplus X \text{ for all } 1 \le i \le k][Aiâ€‹=Aiâ€‹âŠ•X for all 1â‰¤iâ‰¤k]
(Here, âŠ•âŠ•âŠ• denotes bitwise XOR.)

Your goal is to make **all elements of the array pairwise distinct** using the **minimum number of operations**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input:

The first line contains an integer NNN â€” the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,â€¦,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1â€‹,A2â€‹,â€¦,ANâ€‹ â€” the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer â€” the **minimum number of operations** required to make all elements of AAA distinct.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2â‹…1052\cdot 10^52â‹…105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, one of the possible sequence of operations:

Apply âŠ•\oplusâŠ• operation to prefix of length k=1k = 1k=1 with X=7X= 7X=7. The new array becomes [7,0][7, 0][7,0] containing all pairwise distinct elements.



For the second test case, all elements are already pairwise distinct. So no operations are needed.


For the third test case, one of the possible sequence of operations:

Apply âŠ•\oplusâŠ• operation to prefix of length k=2k = 2k=2 with X=3X= 3X=3. The new array becomes [3,2,0,1][3, 2, 0, 1][3,2,0,1] containing all pairwise distinct elements.
